root_text.sn55.22:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sakkesu viharati kapilavatthusmiṁ nigrodhārāme.
root_text.sn55.22:1.3: Atha kho mahānāmo sakko yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho mahānāmo sakko bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn55.22:1.4: “idaṁ, bhante, kapilavatthu iddhañceva phītañca bāhujaññaṁ ākiṇṇamanussaṁ sambādhabyūhaṁ.
root_text.sn55.22:1.5: So khvāhaṁ, bhante, bhagavantaṁ vā payirupāsitvā manobhāvanīye vā bhikkhū sāyanhasamayaṁ kapilavatthuṁ pavisanto;
root_text.sn55.22:1.11: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhante, tasmiṁ samaye mussateva bhagavantaṁ ārabbha sati, mussati dhammaṁ ārabbha sati, mussati saṅghaṁ ārabbha sati.
root_text.sn55.22:1.13: ‘imamhi cāhaṁ samaye kālaṁ kareyyaṁ, kā mayhaṁ gati, ko abhisamparāyo’”ti?
root_text.sn55.22:2.2: Apāpakaṁ te maraṇaṁ bhavissati apāpikā kālaṅkiriyā.
root_text.sn55.22:2.3: Catūhi kho, mahānāma, dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako nibbānaninno hoti nibbānapoṇo nibbānapabbhāro.
root_text.sn55.22:2.5: Idha, mahānāma, ariyasāvako buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—
root_text.sn55.22:2.6: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.22:2.9: ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgato hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn55.22:3.1: Seyyathāpi, mahānāma, rukkho pācīnaninno pācīnapoṇo pācīnapabbhāro, so mūlacchinno katamena papateyyā”ti?
root_text.sn55.22:3.2: “Yena, bhante, ninno yena poṇo yena pabbhāro”ti.
root_text.sn55.22:3.3: “Evameva kho, mahānāma, imehi catūhi dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako nibbānaninno hoti nibbānapoṇo nibbānapabbhāro”ti.

translation_text.sn55.22:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Sakyans, near Kapilavatthu in the Banyan Tree Monastery.
translation_text.sn55.22:1.3: Then Mahānāma the Sakyan went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn55.22:1.4: “Sir, this Kapilavatthu is successful and prosperous, populous, full of people, with cramped cul-de-sacs.
translation_text.sn55.22:1.5: In the late afternoon, after paying homage to the Buddha or an esteemed mendicant, I enter Kapilavatthu.
translation_text.sn55.22:1.11: At that time I lose mindfulness regarding the Buddha, the teaching, and the Saṅgha.
translation_text.sn55.22:1.13: ‘If I were to die at this time, where would I be reborn in my next life?’”
translation_text.sn55.22:2.1: “Do not fear, Mahānāma, do not fear!
translation_text.sn55.22:2.2: Your death will not be a bad one; your passing will not be a bad one.
translation_text.sn55.22:2.3: A noble disciple who has four things slants, slopes, and inclines towards extinguishment.
translation_text.sn55.22:2.5: It’s when a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.22:2.9: And they have the ethical conduct loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.
translation_text.sn55.22:3.1: Suppose there was a tree that slants, slopes, and inclines to the east. If it was cut off at the root where would it fall?”
translation_text.sn55.22:3.2: “Sir, it would fall in the direction that it slants, slopes, and inclines.”
translation_text.sn55.22:3.3: “In the same way, a noble disciple who has four things slants, slopes, and inclines towards extinguishment.”
